problem: Andy the Ant lives on a coordinate plane and is currently at (-20, 20) facing east (positive x-direction). Andy moves 1 unit and then turns 90 degrees left. Andy continues his progress, increasing his distance each time by 1 unit and always turning left. What is the location of the point where Andy makes the 2020th left turn?

problem: Steve wrote the digits 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 in order repeatedly from left to right, forming a list of 10,000 digits, beginning 123451234512... He then erased every third digit from his list (the 3rd, 6th, 9th, ... digits), then erased every fourth digit from the resulting list, and then erased every fifth digit from what remained. What is the sum of the three digits that were then in the positions 2019, 2020, 2021?

problem: Bela and Jenn play the following game on the closed interval [0, n] of the real number line, where n is a fixed integer greater than 4. They take turns playing, with Bela going first. At his first turn, Bela chooses any real number in the interval [0, n]. Thereafter, the player whose turn it is chooses a real number that is more than one unit away from all numbers previously chosen by either player. A player unable to choose such a number loses. Using optimal strategy, which player will win the game?
choices: Bela will win if and only if n is odd.
Jenn will win if and only if n is odd.
Jenn will win if and only if n > 8.
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: There are 10 people standing equally spaced around a circle. Each person knows exactly 3 of the other 9 people: the 2 people standing next to him or her, as well as the person directly across the circle. How many ways are there for the 10 people to split up into 5 pairs so that the members of each pair know each other?

problem: An urn contains one red ball and one blue ball. A box of extra red and blue balls lies nearby. George performs the following operation four times: he draws a ball from the urn at random and then takes a ball of the same color from the box and returns those two matching balls to the urn. After the four iterations the urn contains six balls. What is the probability that the urn contains three balls of each color?

problem: All the high schools in a school district are involved in a fundraiser selling T-shirts. Which of the following is logically equivalent to the statement 'No school bigger than Euclid HS sold more T-shirts than Euclid HS'?

No school that sold more T-shirts than Euclid HS is bigger than Euclid HS.
All schools bigger than Euclid HS sold fewer T-shirts than Euclid HS.
All schools that sold fewer T-shirts than Euclid HS are smaller than Euclid HS.
All schools smaller than Euclid HS sold more T-shirts than Euclid HS.

problem: What is the least positive integer m such that m * 2! * 3! * 4! * 5! * ... * 16! is a perfect square?

problem: Define a positive integer n to be an 'upno' if every pair of consecutive digits of n, read left to right, is strictly increasing, and define a positive integer to be a 'downno' if every pair of consecutive digits, read left to right, is strictly decreasing. Let U equal the total number of upnos and D equal the total number of downnos. What is |U - D|?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A rectangular box P has distinct edge lengths a, b, and c. The sum of the lengths of all 12 edges of P is 13, the sum of the areas of all 6 faces of P is 11/2, and the volume of P is 1/2. What is the length of the longest interior diagonal of P?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Suppose a/14 + b/15 = c/210, where a, b, and c are positive integers. Consider: I. If gcd(a,14)=1 or gcd(b,15)=1, then gcd(c,210)=1. II. If gcd(c,210)=1, then gcd(a,14)=1 or gcd(b,15)=1. III. gcd(c,210)=1 if and only if gcd(a,14)=gcd(b,15)=1. Which are true?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Sonya the frog randomly chooses a landing point uniformly in [0,6] x [0,6] and hops there. She then randomly chooses a distance from [0,1] and one of four directions (north, south, east, west) each with probability 1/4. What is the probability she lands outside the square after the second hop?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Four congruent semicircles are drawn on the surface of a sphere of radius 2. Each semicircle has its diameter as a diameter of the sphere, and the endpoints of all diameters are the same two points. The four semicircular arcs divide the surface into two congruent regions. The length of the curve is pi * sqrt(n). What is n?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Each of 2023 balls is randomly placed into one of 3 bins. Which of the following is closest to the probability that each of the three bins will contain an odd number of balls?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: How many distinct values of x satisfy floor(x)^2 - 3x + 2 = 0, where floor(x) denotes the greatest integer not exceeding x?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: An arithmetic sequence of positive integers has n >= 3 terms, initial term a, and common difference d > 1. Carl found the sum of all terms but inadvertently wrote one term incorrectly, off by 1. The incorrect sum was 222. How many possible values are there for a + d + n?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: What is the perimeter of the boundary of the region consisting of all points which can be expressed as (2u - 3w, v + 4w) with 0 <= u <= 1, 0 <= v <= 1, 0 <= w <= 1?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A regular pentagon with area 1 + sqrt(5) is printed on paper and cut out. All five vertices are folded to the center, creating a smaller pentagon. What is the area of the new smaller pentagon?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A 350-gram bag of coffee beans requires 20 grams of beans per large mug of coffee. What is the greatest number of large mugs of coffee that can be brewed from one bag?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Jerry wrote down the ones digit of each of the first 2025 positive perfect squares: 1, 4, 9, 6, 5, 6, 9, 4, 1, 0, ... What is the sum of all the digits Jerry wrote down?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A triangle whose rows begin and end with special values: the first row is 10, the second row is 10 and 1, and each subsequent row starts with 10, ends with 1, and each interior entry is the sum of the two entries directly above it. What is the sum of the digits of the sum of the numbers in the 11th row?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: The two-digit number ab in base 7 has the same value as the two-digit number ba in base 9. What is a + b?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: In triangle ABC, AB = 10, AC = 18, and angle B = 130 degrees. Let O be the center of the circumscribed circle. What is the degree measure of angle CAO?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: The line y = (1/3)x + 1 divides the square region 0 <= x <= 2, 0 <= y <= 2 into an upper region and a lower region. The vertical line x = a further divides the lower region into two regions of equal area. Then a can be written in the form sqrt(s) - t, where s and t are positive integers. What is s + t?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Frances is standing 15 meters due south of a locked gate. A box of chocolates is x meters due east of the locked gate. An unlocked gate is 9 meters due east of the box, and a second unlocked gate is 8 meters due west of the locked gate. The total distance walked via either unlocked gate to reach the box is the same. What is x?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Emmy ordered 36 custom sweatshirts for her club. The total cost was $ABB.BA, where A and B represent digits and A is not 0. What is A + B?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: How many ordered triples of integers (x, y, z) satisfy the system: -x - y - z <= -2, -x + y + z <= 2, x - y + z <= 2, and x + y - z <= 2?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Let f(n) = n^3 - 5n^2 + 2n + 8 and g(n) = n^3 - 6n^2 + 5n + 12. What is the sum of all integers n such that f(n)/g(n) is defined and is an integer?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: On Monday, 6 students were each randomly assigned to one of 6 tutors, one student per tutor. On Tuesday, the same 6 students and 6 tutors were present, and students were again randomly assigned one per tutor. What is the probability that exactly 2 of the 6 students had the same tutor on both days?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: An equilateral triangle, a rhombus with 60-degree angles, and a regular hexagon each contain the maximum number of mutually tangent nonoverlapping congruent circular disks. Let T, R, and H denote the ratio of total disk area to polygon area for each. Which is true?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: In a right triangle with angles 30, 60, and 90 degrees, the altitude to the hypotenuse has length h. The median to the shortest side divides the altitude into two segments of lengths x and y, where x < y. What is x/(x + y)?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Nine athletes with distinct heights draw wristbands from a bag containing 3 blue, 3 red, and 3 green wristbands. The tallest athlete with each color band becomes that team's captain. What is the probability that the three tallest athletes overall are the three captains?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: The sum from k = 1 to infinity of 1/(k^3 + 6k^2 + 8k) can be expressed as a/b, where a and b are relatively prime positive integers. What is a + b?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A circle is divided into 6 equal sectors by 3 diameters. The sectors are painted, two red, two green, and two blue, such that no two adjacent sectors are the same color. How many distinct colorings are possible?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A strictly decreasing sequence of n positive integers x_1 > x_2 > ... > x_n has the property that the average of the first 3 terms equals 2025, and for each 4 <= k <= n, the average of the first k terms is 1 less than the average of the first k - 1 terms. What is the greatest possible value of n?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: What is the ones digit of floor(sqrt(1)) + floor(sqrt(2)) + ... + floor(sqrt(2025))?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A container has a bottom that is a 1 x 1 square and an open top that is a 3 x 3 square. The four sides are congruent trapezoids. Water from a hose fills the container to the midline in 35 minutes. How many more minutes does it take to fill the rest?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Four congruent semicircles are inscribed in a unit square, each with diameter on one of the four sides. One endpoint of each diameter is a vertex of the square, and adjacent semicircles are tangent to each other. A small circle at the center of the square is tangent to all four semicircles. The diameter of the small circle can be written as (sqrt(a) + b)(sqrt(c) + d). What is a + b + c + d?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Each unit square in a 3 x 3 grid is colored red, blue, or yellow so that each red square shares an edge with at least one blue square, each blue square shares an edge with at least one yellow square, and each yellow square shares an edge with at least one red square. How many such colorings are there, counting rotations and reflections of the same coloring only once?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A seven-digit positive integer n is chosen at random. What is the probability that n is divisible by 11, given that the sum of its digits is 61?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Horace and Vera each fill a 141 x 91 grid with integers 1 through 12831. Horace fills row by row (left to right, top to bottom). Vera fills column by column (top to bottom, left to right). How many squares receive the same number from both?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: A frog starts at position 0. From 0, it moves to 1 with probability 1/2 or disappears with probability 1/2. From any position n >= 1, it moves to n+1 with probability 1/4, to n-1 with probability 1/4, or disappears with probability 1/2. What is the probability it reaches position 4?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.

problem: Square ABCD has side length 4. Point P lies on side AD with AP = 8/5, and point Q lies on side CD with DQ = 10/3. A light beam is shot from P toward Q. When the beam hits a side, it reflects with equal angles of incidence and reflection. At which vertex does the beam eventually terminate?
solution: See AoPS wiki for detailed solution.
